title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: Karnataka Class 10
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A farmer in Karnataka borrows ₹50,000 from a cooperative bank at 9% per annum simple interest for 2 years. What is the total amount he repays?
questions.explanation: SI = (50000 × 9 × 2)/100 = ₹9,000. Total = ₹59,000. Karnataka's PCARD (Primary Agricultural Credit and Rural Development) offers crop loans at these rates. Agricultural loans are critical to farmers' livelihoods.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A Bengaluru tech employee earns ₹80,000/month. She invests 30% in SIP mutual fund monthly. Using AP, after 12 months with d = ₹500 increase per month (salary hike), what is her total investment in year 1?
questions.explanation: Month 1: 30% of 80000=₹24000. Monthly increase in investment: 30% of ₹500=₹150. AP: a=24000, d=150, n=12. Sn = 12/2 × (2×24000 + 11×150) = 6×(48000+1650) = 6×49650 = ₹2,97,900. Wait, this changes with salary increase. Initial SIP = 30% of month's salary. Total = 12×24000 + 150×(0+1+...+11) = 288000 + 150×66 = 288000+9900 = ₹2,97,900. Closest to option b.
questions.subject: Mathematics
